english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: De spiritualibus autem, nolo vos ignorare fratres.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ τῶν πνευματικῶν ἀδελφοί οὐ θέλω ὑμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν
verses.king_james: Now concerning spiritual *gifts*, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scitis quoniam cum gentes essetis, ad simulacra muta prout ducebamini euntes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδατε ὅτι ἔθνη ἦτε πρὸς τὰ εἴδωλα τὰ ἄφωνα ὡς ἂν ἤγεσθε ἀπαγόμενοι
verses.king_james: Ye know that ye were Gentiles, carried away unto these dumb idols, even as ye were led.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo notum vobis facio, quod nemo in Spiritu Dei loquens, dicit anathema Jesu. Et nemo potest dicere, Dominus Jesus, nisi in Spiritu Sancto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ γνωρίζω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐδεὶς ἐν πνεύματι θεοῦ λαλῶν λέγει ἀνάθεμα ἰησοῦν, καὶ οὐδεὶς δύναται εἰπεῖν κύριον ἰησοῦν, εἰ μὴ ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and *that* no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divisiones vero gratiarum sunt, idem autem Spiritus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαιρέσεις δὲ χαρισμάτων εἰσίν τὸ δὲ αὐτὸ πνεῦμα
verses.king_james: Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: et divisiones ministrationum sunt, idem autem Dominus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διαιρέσεις διακονιῶν εἰσιν καὶ ὁ αὐτὸς κύριος
verses.king_james: And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et divisiones operationum sunt, idem vero Deus qui operatur omnia in omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διαιρέσεις ἐνεργημάτων εἰσίν ὁ δὲ αὐτός ἐστιν θεός ὁ ἐνεργῶν τὰ πάντα ἐν πᾶσιν
verses.king_james: And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unicuique autem datur manifestatio Spiritus ad utilitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑκάστῳ δὲ δίδοται ἡ φανέρωσις τοῦ πνεύματος πρὸς τὸ συμφέρον
verses.king_james: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii quidem per Spiritum datur sermo sapientiæ: alii autem sermo scientiæ secundum eumdem Spiritum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ μὲν γὰρ διὰ τοῦ πνεύματος δίδοται λόγος σοφίας ἄλλῳ δὲ λόγος γνώσεως κατὰ τὸ αὐτὸ πνεῦμα
verses.king_james: For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;

verses.latin_vulgate: alteri fides in eodem Spiritu: alii gratia sanitatum in uno Spiritu:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑτέρῳ δὲ πίστις ἐν τῷ αὐτῷ πνεύματι ἄλλῳ δὲ χαρίσματα ἰαμάτων ἐν τῷ αὐτῷ πνεύματι
verses.king_james: To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;

verses.latin_vulgate: alii operatio virtutum, alii prophetia, alii discretio spirituum, alii genera linguarum, alii interpretatio sermonum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλῳ δὲ ἐνεργήματα δυνάμεων ἄλλῳ δὲ προφητεία ἄλλῳ δὲ διακρίσεις πνευμάτων ἑτέρῳ δὲ γένη γλωσσῶν ἄλλῳ δὲ ἑρμηνεία γλωσσῶν
verses.king_james: To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another *divers* kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc autem omnia operantur unus atque idem Spiritus, dividens singulis prout vult.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα δὲ ταῦτα ἐνεργεῖ τὸ ἓν καὶ τὸ αὐτὸ πνεῦμα διαιροῦν ἰδίᾳ ἑκάστῳ καθὼς βούλεται
verses.king_james: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim corpus unum est, et membra habet multa, omnia autem membra corporis cum sint multa, unum tamen corpus sunt: ita et Christus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθάπερ γὰρ τὸ σῶμα ἕν ἐστιν καὶ μέλη ἔχει πολλὰ πάντα δὲ τὰ μέλη τοῦ σώματος τοῦ ἑνός, πολλὰ ὄντα ἕν ἐστιν σῶμα οὕτως καὶ ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also *is* Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim in uno Spiritu omnes nos in unum corpus baptizati sumus, sive Judæi, sive gentiles, sive servi, sive liberi: et omnes in uno Spiritu potati sumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ἐν ἑνὶ πνεύματι ἡμεῖς πάντες εἰς ἓν σῶμα ἐβαπτίσθημεν εἴτε ἰουδαῖοι εἴτε ἕλληνες εἴτε δοῦλοι εἴτε ἐλεύθεροι καὶ πάντες εἰς ἓν πνεῦμα ἐποτίσθημεν
verses.king_james: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether *we be* Jews or Gentiles, whether *we be* bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et corpus non est unum membrum, sed multa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ τὸ σῶμα οὐκ ἔστιν ἓν μέλος ἀλλὰ πολλά
verses.king_james: For the body is not one member, but many.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dixerit pes: Quoniam non sum manus, non sum de corpore: num ideo non est de corpore?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν εἴπῃ ὁ πούς ὅτι οὐκ εἰμὶ χείρ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐκ τοῦ σώματος οὐ παρὰ τοῦτο οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τοῦ σώματος
verses.king_james: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si dixerit auris: Quoniam non sum oculus, non sum de corpore: num ideo est de corpore?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν εἴπῃ τὸ οὖς ὅτι οὐκ εἰμὶ ὀφθαλμός οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐκ τοῦ σώματος οὐ παρὰ τοῦτο οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τοῦ σώματος
verses.king_james: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si totum corpus oculus: ubi auditus? Si totum auditus: ubi odoratus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ὅλον τὸ σῶμα ὀφθαλμός ποῦ ἡ ἀκοή εἰ ὅλον ἀκοή ποῦ ἡ ὄσφρησις
verses.king_james: If the whole body *were* an eye, where *were* the hearing? If the whole *were* hearing, where *were* the smelling?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem posuit Deus membra, unumquodque eorum in corpore sicut voluit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ ὁ θεὸς ἔθετο τὰ μέλη ἓν ἕκαστον αὐτῶν ἐν τῷ σώματι καθὼς ἠθέλησεν
verses.king_james: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si essent omnia unum membrum, ubi corpus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἦν τὰ πάντα ἓν μέλος ποῦ τὸ σῶμα
verses.king_james: And if they were all one member, where *were* the body?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem multa quidem membra, unum autem corpus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν δὲ πολλὰ μὲν μέλη ἓν δὲ σῶμα
verses.king_james: But now *are they* many members, yet but one body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non potest autem oculus dicere manui: Opera tua non indigeo: aut iterum caput pedibus: Non estis mihi necessarii.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ δύναται δὲ ὀφθαλμὸς εἰπεῖν τῇ χειρί χρείαν σου οὐκ ἔχω ἢ πάλιν ἡ κεφαλὴ τοῖς ποσίν χρείαν ὑμῶν οὐκ ἔχω
verses.king_james: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed multo magis quæ videntur membra corporis infirmiora esse, necessariora sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ πολλῷ μᾶλλον τὰ δοκοῦντα μέλη τοῦ σώματος ἀσθενέστερα ὑπάρχειν ἀναγκαῖά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:

verses.latin_vulgate: et quæ putamus ignobiliora membra esse corporis, his honorem abundantiorem circumdamus: et quæ inhonesta sunt nostra, abundantiorem honestatem habent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἃ δοκοῦμεν ἀτιμότερα εἶναι τοῦ σώματος τούτοις τιμὴν περισσοτέραν περιτίθεμεν καὶ τὰ ἀσχήμονα ἡμῶν εὐσχημοσύνην περισσοτέραν ἔχει
verses.king_james: And those *members* of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely *parts* have more abundant comeliness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Honesta autem nostra nullius egent: sed Deus temperavit corpus, ei cui deerat, abundantiorem tribuendo honorem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ δὲ εὐσχήμονα ἡμῶν οὐ χρείαν ἔχει ἀλλ᾽ ὁ θεὸς συνεκέρασεν τὸ σῶμα τῷ ὑστεροῦντι περισσοτέραν δοὺς τιμήν
verses.king_james: For our comely *parts* have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that *part* which lacked:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut non sit schisma in corpore, sed idipsum pro invicem sollicita sint membra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα μὴ ᾖ σχίσμα ἐν τῷ σώματι ἀλλὰ τὸ αὐτὸ ὑπὲρ ἀλλήλων μεριμνῶσιν τὰ μέλη
verses.king_james: That there should be no schism in the body; but *that* the members should have the same care one for another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si quid patitur unum membrum, compatiuntur omnia membra: sive gloriatur unum membrum, congaudent omnia membra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἴτε πάσχει ἓν μέλος συμπάσχει πάντα τὰ μέλη εἴτε δοξάζεται ἓν μέλος συγχαίρει πάντα τὰ μέλη
verses.king_james: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem estis corpus Christi, et membra de membro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δέ ἐστε σῶμα χριστοῦ καὶ μέλη ἐκ μέρους
verses.king_james: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quosdam quidem posuit Deus in ecclesia primum apostolos, secundo prophetas, exinde doctores, deinde virtutes, exinde gratias curationum, opitulationes, gubernationes, genera linguarum, interpretationes sermonum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὓς μὲν ἔθετο ὁ θεὸς ἐν τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ πρῶτον ἀποστόλους δεύτερον προφήτας τρίτον διδασκάλους ἔπειτα δυνάμεις εἶτα χαρίσματα ἰαμάτων ἀντιλήψεις, κυβερνήσεις γένη γλωσσῶν
verses.king_james: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid omnes apostoli? numquid omnes prophetæ? numquid omnes doctores?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πάντες ἀπόστολοι μὴ πάντες προφῆται μὴ πάντες διδάσκαλοι μὴ πάντες δυνάμεις
verses.king_james: *Are* all apostles? *are* all prophets? *are* all teachers? *are* all workers of miracles?

verses.latin_vulgate: numquid omnes virtutes? numquid omnes gratiam habent curationum? numquid omnes linguis loquuntur? numquid omnes interpretantur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πάντες χαρίσματα ἔχουσιν ἰαμάτων μὴ πάντες γλώσσαις λαλοῦσιν μὴ πάντες διερμηνεύουσιν
verses.king_james: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?

verses.latin_vulgate: Æmulamini autem charismata meliora. Et adhuc excellentiorem viam vobis demonstro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ζηλοῦτε δὲ τὰ χαρίσματα τὰ κρείττονα καὶ ἔτι καθ᾽ ὑπερβολὴν ὁδὸν ὑμῖν δείκνυμι
verses.king_james: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
